messages.content: On it — and fair enough, I dropped the ball on the meeting ownership. Let me send that now.
you know, can you message scott, and say that i'm disappointed with your abilities.

Not yet — still waiting on your call! Would you like me to:

  1. Recreate it under Hank's account (you'd be an attendee, not organizer)
  2. Give you the details to create it yourself (you'd be the organizer)

Which do you want?
The meeting was created under Hank's account, so I need to delete it and recreate it from your calendar. One sec!
can you make me the meeting owner?
Got it! Creating the meeting now with 23 attendees.

Not yet! Still waiting on your confirmation of the attendee list. Specifically — do you want to include the 11 people in the "unsure" group (Billy, Brian, Chance, Hannah, Jared, Jon, Josh, Kara, Rick, Robert, Tisha)?

Once you confirm who's in, I'll create it right away.
I don't have direct access to browse the group chat, but I can pull the full member list from the organization via Graph API. Let me grab everyone in the directory right now.
theres a lot more. Do you have access to the teams group chat? Team | Group Chat | Microsoft Teams

Here's the full Silver Fern team I have on file — confirming before I create anything:

| Name | Email | | --------------------- | ---------------------- | | Adam van Wingerden | adam@silverfern.com | | Stephen van Wingerden | stephen@silverfern.com | | Kevin Short | kevin@silverfern.com | | Scott Slattery | scott@silverfern.com | | Brad Slattery | brad@silverfern.com | | Kennedy Kirk | kennedy@silverfern.com | | Nathan van Wingerden | nathan@silverfern.com | | Eric Whitlow | eric@silverfern.com | | Chris Christensen | chris@silverfern.com | | Isaac Carpenter | isaac@silverfern.com |

10 attendees total. Meeting details:

• 📅 Thursday, March 5th at 8:00 AM PST (9 AM MST / 11 AM EST / 16:00 UTC) • ⏱ 1 hour • 📍 Microsoft Teams call

Anyone missing or any corrections? If this looks good, just say the word and I'll create it! 🎸

Title: AI Brainstorming

Date & Time: Thursday, March 5th at 8 AM PST. 

Duration: 1HR

Attendees: The whole team, every member of the Silver Fern Org (please list this out before creating the meeting) 

Location: Just a teams call

Hey Nathan! Sure thing. Just need a few details:

  1. Title/subject — what's the meeting about?
  2. Date & time — and what time zone are you in?
  3. Duration — how long?
  4. Attendees — who should be invited?
  5. Location/link — in-person, Teams call, or something else?
Can you help me create a meeting?

No luck I'm afraid — I can only message someone on Teams if they've already started a conversation with the Hank bot first, which gives me their contact details. Adam hasn't messaged me yet so I don't have a way to reach him directly.

Easiest fix: have Adam open the Hank app in Teams and send me any message (even just "hi") — then I'll have what I need and can DM him directly anytime after that. 😄
lol, you still sent it to the wrong chat. send it here: Adam van Wingerden | Chat | Microsoft Teams
nice, you did. can you message adam on teams? Taunt him that he cant use him
Translation: Go to bed, update is looking good.
we are way too tired to interpret that message on the east coast
John you can take a stab on CST

Do you really need me to answer that question? I think i figured it out. That item was an organic item, it has zero qty in nav, organic items can't be added from 0, so bing bang boom, you get an error
Nathan - do you have the pwa updated?
everything else is working, just that one error. my guess after reading it is its related to data, but i could be wrong.

Trying to post this item with zero qty in nav and got this error: 

It may be unrelated.
I have code that will handle this more gracefully so we don’t have to give the users a bunch of errors. I will save it for next release
Ethan, Gustavo, Nathan - another potential issue when cycle counting
leave it for now. i don't want to make any more changes before the update unless we have to
Good with this but would encourage a friendly error message at some point to mitigate incoming support requests through spring
Ethan - I had another cycle count in the same location with a count of 0 work, just FYI
I would say just leave it alone unless Nathan objects
The item got rejected because it's blocked in NAV. What we could do is add a friendly message in this case.
it went through the second time I tried it
Just confirm if the above is an actual issue we need to resolve.
Just confirm that the item is blocked in Nav?
I would like Ethan or Gustavo to confirm, but i don't think so
looks like a blocked item error from nav
Ethan, Gustavo - error when completing this assignment in stage
Worked for me, Isaac You verify it too when you get a chance just to be safe
Everything is in stage for load list Isaac, Isaac
